patch.value: Implement a first-class pm mutation for redacting sensitive strings from append-only history streams without hand-editing JSONL files, preserving replay integrity by recomputing affected history hashes or recording a verifiable redaction marker.

A supported pm command can redact configured literals/patterns from item files and history streams; history drift validation remains meaningful after redaction; redaction operations are themselves auditable; secret scans can include history streams without false failures from known private operations paths.

value.author: codex-audit
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm search history stream redaction command redact private path history drift --limit 10 found only closed history/security items; current audit discovered remaining private remote paths in historical JSONL streams after current item docs were sanitized.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Additional hardening completed in this remediation run: scripts/check-secrets now detects absolute home-path and user@private-host leakage patterns (with test/example exclusions), and Sentry scrub logic now redacts nested path-bearing payload fields so newly emitted events store [scrubbed_path] instead of local filesystem paths. This supports current-tree direct scrub governance while a first-class history redaction command remains open scope.

Known history drift remains on pm-54d, pm-7vm9, pm-f3q4, pm-fdla, pm-mb4n, and pm-tq1; keeping this item as canonical rather than creating a duplicate.
